steps.title: Identify the timeline paradox
steps.content: The DNA analysis found Polynesian sweet potato varieties diverged from South American ones over 100,000 years ago, yet Polynesia was peopled only 3,000 years ago. This 97,000-year gap—the varieties were genetically distinct long before humans could have arrived—is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Conclude what human activity couldn't explain
steps.content:
Human activity could not have introduced the plant, since the genetic divergence predates human settlement by roughly 97,000 years.

The Polynesian varieties must have arrived through natural means.
  • A contradicts the passage's South American origin
  • B claims recent introduction despite the DNA evidence
  • D addresses domestication rather than introduction
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
